explanation.text: Paragraph 5 describes the island’s terrain as being covered in snow, referring to the ground and landscape. This context shows that terrain means the surface features of the land.

The project uses Devon Island’s Mars-like conditions so people can experience and study an environment similar to the Red Planet. Therefore, gaining an idea of what Mars is like best reflects the project’s central goal.

Paragraphs 3 through 5 explain which Devon Island conditions resemble Mars and which do not. The later statement about making Mars exploration successful and safe supports this idea because it explains why scientists study those conditions.

Paragraph 11 says the Haughton scientists are working to ensure humans are ready for a Mars mission. Their practice with spacesuits, rovers, samples, and a greenhouse supports that point because those are tasks astronauts may perform.

Devon Island is a frigid desert covered in snow for most of the year, and even its summer average is only about 34°F. These details show that year-round cold makes the island unsuitable for permanent residents.

Robot missions found possible frozen water, suggesting that tiny life may have existed on Mars or may remain there. This shows why the missions raised questions that future human exploration could help answer.

Pascal Lee founded the Haughton Mars Project in 1997 and has returned every summer since. Because he directs and has long participated in the work, his quotations provide knowledgeable explanations throughout the article.

Paragraph 4 introduces the letter offering Pappa work in Iowa, the event that starts the move. Paragraph 14 explains its effect because Seema’s four-person household feels broken away from the larger family.
Seema wants to reassure Raju but immediately wonders how she possibly can. This shows her uncertainty and sadness about leaving him, rather than merely stating facts about their school routine.
Seema is frightened about the move, but she does not tell Pappa because he is so happy about going to America. This shows that she holds back her fears to avoid spoiling his excited mood.

The story’s central conflict is Seema’s fear and sadness about moving to America away from her close extended family. Her wondering how she can leave them therefore belongs in a summary of the main events.

Seema explains that the grandparents, parents, aunt, uncle, and cousins all live in one house and eat from one kitchen. This shared home best demonstrates how closely all members of the extended family live together.
Raju says he hates the year of the move, while Seema worries about leaving, cannot discuss her fears, and sleeps poorly. These reactions show that major change can be difficult to accept.

People use facial expressions to communicate, and the researcher says such expressions develop trust and understanding. Therefore, giving robots humanlike expressions can create a better connection with the people beside them.

The article describes robots vacuuming, mowing lawns, cleaning toys, checking email, and performing other routine chores. Because robots can handle useful daily tasks, households have practical reasons to adopt them widely.

Nao can clean toys, play games, talk, walk, recognize people and places, and assist with tasks such as checking email. This variety of uses supports its likely popularity because it can meet many household needs.

Nexi communicates with speech and expressions, Nao talks and remembers faces and voices, and RIBA recognizes people and responds to commands. These shared abilities show that all three interact and communicate with humans.

The phrase introduces innovative water robots that resemble fish, clams, rays, lobsters, and penguins and perform unusual jobs. Because these inventions are noteworthy, “making a splash” means creating excitement or attracting attention.

The robotic fish can search beneath the water for submerged ships and oil or gas pipelines. This shows a robot performing deep underwater work that would be difficult or dangerous for a human to carry out directly.

Across its examples, the article emphasizes robots that communicate, recognize people, respond to commands, and assist with useful work. Therefore, the broad idea that humanlike robots will work well with people is most important for a summary.
